Steelcase Gesture vs Leap Plus

Comparing Steelcase's two flagship options for tall users

Overview

The Steelcase Gesture and Steelcase Leap Plus are both premium ergonomic chairs, but they are designed to solve different problems for tall users. The Gesture emphasizes arm mobility and posture flexibility, while the Leap Plus focuses on size accommodation, deeper seat adjustment, and higher weight capacity.

This comparison looks at real-world fit factors—seat dimensions, adjustability, support feel, and overall usability—to help tall users determine which chair better matches their body type and working style.

Dimensional Comparison

The Steelcase Gesture typically offers a seat height range of approximately 16 to 21 inches and is designed to support a wide variety of seated postures. Its seat depth adjustment works well for many tall users, but the overall platform remains closer to standard sizing with a strong emphasis on upper-body ergonomics and arm movement.

The Steelcase Leap Plus is built for users who need more physical room and adjustment margin. It provides a wider seat, deeper seat depth adjustment—often cited up to roughly 19.75 inches—and a much higher weight capacity, commonly listed up to 500 lbs. This makes it a better fit when standard chair dimensions feel restrictive.

For Tall Users

For tall users with larger frames or those who regularly feel constrained by standard office chairs, the Leap Plus is generally the safer choice. Its larger seat platform and deeper adjustability help support longer legs and reduce the "perched" feeling that can occur when seat depth is too shallow.

The Gesture is often a strong option for tall users with more average builds who prioritize movement and flexibility. Its arm system is particularly well suited for device-heavy workflows, where arm positioning changes frequently throughout the day.

Verdict

Choose the Steelcase Leap Plus if you need maximum size accommodation, deeper seat support, and higher weight capacity for long-term comfort.

Choose the Steelcase Gesture if you are tall with a more average build and value advanced arm mobility and dynamic sitting support.
